title: Ancient DNA Extraction from Chunk Samples
author: Marcel Keller

steps.title: Clean table bench surface with DNA Exitus and wipe dry
steps.instruction: Clean table bench surface with DNA Exitus and wipe dry. Turn the hood on full power and open the glass.

steps.title: Only applicable if the samples have...
steps.instruction: Only applicable if the samples have been dried in the hood previously after decontamination. Move dry samples from the hood to the table bench and put down a new sheet of aluminum foil.

steps.title: Clean hood surface with DNA Exitus...
steps.instruction: Clean hood surface with DNA Exitus and put down a new sheet of aluminum foil. Wipe down reagents with DNA Exitus.

steps.title: Place all reagents (except proteinase K)...
steps.instruction: Place all reagents (except proteinase K) and consumables in the hood and UV while weighing.

steps.title: Only applicable if the samples were...
steps.instruction: Only applicable if the samples were not placed in tubes after drying and weighed before. Tare scale with small weighing boat labeled with the sample number, place tooth/petrous core on the weighing boat with the tweezers and record weight. Change gloves when finished with weighing.

steps.title: Make EDTA 0
steps.instruction: Make EDTA 0.5 Molarity (M) pH 8.0 , Proteinase K 18.18 mg/mL , and tube calculations: A B C D E 1 Sample ID Weight (mg) EDTA (ml) PK (µl) Tube 2 [Sample ID] x 2 ml per 100 mg (x/50) 50 µl per 100 mg (x/2) 5 ml 3 ABC001A 100 2 50 5 4 ABC001A 300 6 150 15

steps.title: Label your tubes for the extractions
steps.instruction: Label your tubes for the extractions: A B C 1 Top Side 2 Project ID PROJ PROJ 3 Sample ID ABC001A ABC001A 4 Extraction # 1 1 5 Initials YZ 6 Date DD/MM/YYYY 7 Content P Pellet

steps.title: Add calculated amount of EDTA to each tube
steps.instruction: Add calculated amount of EDTA to each tube. If the tubes are empty you can use the same pipette tip as long as you do not get liquid into the filter or touch any surfaces with the tip.

steps.title: If necessary
steps.instruction: If necessary: Make Proteinase K solution by adding 5.5 mL PCR clean water to 100 mg of powder. Shake to dissolve. Let sit before using. Store in the freezer at -20 °C .

steps.title: Add the calculated amount of Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Add the calculated amount of Proteinase K to each tube and pipette-mix. Change your tips each time.

steps.title: Add samples to their individual extraction tube
steps.instruction: Add samples to their individual extraction tube.

steps.title: Make sure that the lids are...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the lids are tightly sealed to avoid leakage.

steps.title: Wipe tubes with DNA Exitus, wrap tubes with Parafilm
steps.instruction: Wipe tubes with DNA Exitus, wrap tubes with Parafilm.

steps.title: Place on the nutating mixer for...
steps.instruction: Place on the nutating mixer for 24 rpm, Room temperature, 3 days . Continue with the purification protocol after 72 h.

steps.title: This protocol assumes that the samples...
steps.instruction: This protocol assumes that the samples are drying in the hood after decontamination when you start the protocol. Alternatively, if the hood needs to be cleared between decontamination and extraction, dry samples can be stored in tubes.

steps.title: This protocol is to be followed...
steps.instruction: This protocol is to be followed by the purification protocol after 3 days (72 h). Please plan the purification of the extracts accordingly.

steps.title: Set up decontamination station
steps.instruction: Set up decontamination station: 4 disposable 100 ml beakers 6% NaOCl (bleach) aliquot [2x] MilliQ water [1x] 70% ethanol aliquot [1x]

steps.title: To decontaminate tweezers, soak in 6%...
steps.instruction: To decontaminate tweezers, soak in 6% bleach for 5 minutes, rinse with MilliQ water, rinse with 70% Ethanol.
